07 — Hardest Programs

Top 5 hardest bachelor programs in Armenia

Based on minimum admission scores and applicant competition across 2018–2022, these programs consistently ranked as the most competitive in Armenia.

🥇 RANK 1
General Medicine
Yerevan State Medical University
Score 2022: 92 +4 pts since 2018 ~350 applicants/yr Extremely high competition
Medicine remains the most competitive field in Armenia. Strong demand, limited state-funded seats, and high academic thresholds make it the hardest program to enter.
🥈 RANK 2
Engineering Sciences
American University of Armenia
Score 2022: 90 Rapid applicant growth English-language High tuition barrier
AUA's engineering programs combine international standards with selective admission criteria, driving high minimum scores and growing competition year over year.
🥉 RANK 3
Data Science
Yerevan State University
Score 2022: 88 Consistent upward trend ~120 applicants Strong math requirement
The rapid growth of AI and tech industries has made Data Science one of the most competitive new-generation programs, with scores rising every year.
4️⃣ RANK 4
Business
American University of Armenia
Score 2022: 88 ~200 applicants High international demand
Business programs in English show increasing selectivity, reflecting global career aspirations among Armenian students.
5️⃣ RANK 5
Management
French University in Armenia (UFAR)
Score 2022: 88 Stable competition Dual-degree model
The French-Armenian dual-degree model attracts students seeking European credentials, sustaining consistently high admission thresholds across all five years.

📊 What the data shows across 2018–2022

  • Medical and IT programs dominate the top ranks
  • English-language programs show higher selectivity
  • Technical fields steadily increased their score thresholds
  • Humanities programs remain more accessible
09 — Applicant Journey

Applicant Journey: University Admissions
in Armenia Case Study: Yerevan State University (2020–2025)

From registration to enrollment — this example illustrates how applicants move through the university admission process and where the largest drop-offs occur.

Every year, thousands of Armenian high-school graduates enter the national university admission process. However, not all applicants who begin the process eventually enroll in a university program.

This visualization presents a case study based on data from Yerevan State University, showing how applicants move through the admission pipeline between 2020 and 2025 — from exam participation to final enrollment.

Select a year to explore the flow of applicants through each stage of the admission journey.

Year
